title: calochlaena javanica
intro: Calochlaena javanica, also known as the Queensland tree fern, is a species of fern native to Australia and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the leaves of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever and rheumatism, due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Calochlaena javanica include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function, while also acting as an expectorant to help alleviate coughs and colds. Calochlaena javanica is often prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
